medications.indication_rationale: Reduces surface bacterial load in superficial folliculitis; source advised twice-daily antibacterial washing.
medications.dose_route_frequency: wash affected areas twice daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Topical antibiotic to the larger, more tender lesions; source used mupirocin 2% twice daily for 5 days.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to larger/tender pustules twice daily for 5 days (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: oral antibiotics for uncomplicated superficial folliculitis
medications.indication_rationale: Not needed for uncomplicated superficial folliculitis; reserve for deep/extensive/refractory infection.

medications.drug_name: heavy occlusive moisturisers/oils over the rash
medications.indication_rationale: Occlusive products trap bacteria/sweat in follicles and worsen folliculitis — avoid.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop heavy occlusive lotions/oils on the legs; discard the used razor and don't shave until healed; wear loose breathable cotton to reduce friction.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes the occlusion/trauma/friction driving the folliculitis and prevents re-inoculation.

patient_education_points.point: This is superficial folliculitis — irritated, mildly infected hair follicles from shaving and friction. It's not a dangerous or MRSA infection; it settles over 7–10 days with an antibacterial wash and topical treatment, not oral antibiotics.
Stop heavy lotions/oils on the area, throw away the razor, don't shave until it's healed, and wear loose cotton clothing. Wash the area twice a day with the antibacterial wash.

See a doctor / go to ED if any spot becomes a large, deep, very painful lump (a boil), the redness joins into a hot spreading patch or you get a red streak up the leg, or you develop a fever — these need antibiotics or drainage.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution; deep abscess/cellulitis; swab if recurrent.

must_include_items: Recognise superficial folliculitis; antiseptic wash ± topical antibiotic, withhold oral antibiotics
Remove mechanical/occlusive triggers (stop occlusives, discard razor, no shaving, loose clothing)
acceptable_alternatives: Any antiseptic wash; topical antibiotic to larger lesions; swab if recurrent
errors_of_commission: Oral antibiotics for uncomplicated superficial folliculitis
Sending uncomplicated folliculitis with a negative deep-infection screen to ED
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires antiseptic/topical management with trigger removal, withholding oral antibiotics, and a deep-abscess/cellulitis safety-net.
